title: use_image_resolv_conf in the REST API libpod/containers/create command is ignored
body:
### Issue Description

When using the REST API to create a container, the `use_image_resolv_conf` flag seems to be ingored.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  Execute
```
curl -v --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock \
  -X POST http://d/v4.9/libpod/containers/create \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "Image": "docker.io/library/alpine:latest",
    "name": "test-resolv-conf",
    "command": ["sleep", "10000"],
    "use_image_resolv_conf": true
  }'
```
2. `podman start test-resolv-conf`
3. `podman exec -it test-resolv-conf cat /etc/resolv.conf`

### Describe the results you received

`/etc/resolv.conf` exists and contains a couple of `nameserver` entries

### Describe the results you expected

`/etc/resolv.conf` should not exist in the image, because the alpine image does not contain it and podman should not write it due to the use_image_resolv_conf flag.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

When using `podman create --dns=none`, things work as expected.

I tried `UseImageResolvConf` instead of `use_image_resolv_conf` but got the same result

comments.body: The option is called `use_image_resolve_conf`, notice the "e" in resolve.
